text.title: SOC2 Control to Repo Artifact Map — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every soc2-map.yaml application must enforce. These are HARD rules — auditor or operator preferences cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every in-scope TSC criterion (e.g., CC6.1, CC7.2) MUST have at least one mapped artifact entry; a control with zero artifacts fails CI.
rule.rationale: A control without evidence is functionally absent at audit time; renderer must hard-fail to prevent silent gaps.

rule.statement: Each artifact entry MUST declare type ∈ {file, ci_job, dashboard_url, ticket_query, iac_resource, runbook} and a resolvable locator (repo-relative path, CI job ID, URL, JQL/Linear query, Terraform module address).
rule.rationale: Free-form prose locators ("see Slack #ops") are not auditable; a closed type vocabulary keeps the renderer deterministic.

rule.statement: The map MUST be validated daily by CI: file artifacts resolve to existing paths, ci_job artifacts exist in the pipeline catalogue, URLs return non-404 — failures open a P2 ticket within 24h.
rule.rationale: Annual checks let artifact rot accumulate for 11 months; daily validation catches drift while the fix is cheap.

rule.statement: Each artifact entry MUST declare an owner (Git handle or team identifier) and a review_period (≤365 days); expired ownership blocks merge of unrelated PRs touching the control.
rule.rationale: Auditors disqualify evidence with no current human responsible; review_period forces yearly re-confirmation.

rule.statement: The rendered evidence binder MUST be reproducible: same map + same git ref ⇒ byte-identical binder; renderer pins all upstream tool versions and refuses to run on a dirty working tree.
rule.rationale: Auditors challenge evidence whose origin cannot be re-derived; reproducibility kills the challenge before it starts.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: controls: CC6.1: description: "Logical access security" owner: "@platform-team" review_period_days: 180 artifacts: - type: iac_resource locator: "infra/terraform/iam/policies.tf" - type: ci_job locator: "ci.access-review-monthly" - type: dashboard_url locator: "https://grafana.example.com/d/iam-anomalies"
example.note: Three artifact types, named owner, sub-yearly review. Renderer can verify all three locators in CI; auditor pulls the binder and sees provenance at a glance.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: controls: CC6.1: "We have IAM, ask the platform team"
example.note: No type vocabulary, no resolvable locator, no owner, no review period. The renderer cannot validate anything; the auditor will issue a finding.
